danger_signals.visual_form: Strogg creatures with visible mechanical augmentations, cybernetic limbs, and alien biology
Industrial machinery, steam vents, toxic areas in Strogg bases
danger_signals.meaning: World hazards that damage the player character

danger_signals.visual_form: Visible wounds on enemies after damage taken, damaged walls and terrain
danger_signals.meaning: Combat is occurring or has recently occurred; environment is unstable

danger_signals.visual_form: Heavy defensive structures, bunkers, defensive positions
danger_signals.meaning: High-threat zone requiring tactical approach or air-strike objectives

safety_signals.visual_form: Captured human comrades, visually distinct from Strogg
safety_signals.meaning: Friendly presence; objective location or narrative checkpoint

safety_signals.visual_form: Invulnerability, Enviro-Suit, Rebreather, Ammo Pack, Bandolier pickups
Safe zones connecting levels with traversal capability
safety_signals.meaning: Strategic waypoints for mission planning and objective completion

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Space marine armor and human weapons (Shotgun, Machine Gun, Rocket Launcher, Railgun, BFG 10K)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Military bases, human technology, captured comrades as narrative markers

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Half-mutant half-machine hybrid creatures with visible cybernetic augmentations
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy types distinguished by augmentation patterns and combat roles (Tank Commanders with removable heads as mission objectives)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Stroggos homeplanet structures, industrial alien architecture, mechanical and biological fusion in base design

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Active military invasion by humans against Strogg homeplanet
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Large outdoor combat areas, bunker installations, defensive fortifications, military objective markers (tank commander heads, air-strike targets)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Strogg enemies actively defend territory; humans conduct mission-based tactical operations with specific objectives

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Strogg occupation and assimilation of captured humans
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Non-hostile human NPCs present as insane captives within Strogg facilities
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Insane comrades cannot be interacted with; represent stakes of the conflict and narrative progression

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Player character isolated as last squad survivor
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Single protagonist (Bitterman) navigating alone through enemy territory
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Solo mission-based gameplay structure requiring completion of strategic objectives without squad support

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Textures and environmental design emphasizing mechanical and alien hybrid aesthetics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies demonstrate damage states with visible wounds; show physiological responses to player actions

environmental_storytelling.technique: Narrative cutscenes tied to mission objectives
environmental_storytelling.example: CGI cutscenes showing player progress (stealing Tank Commander heads, calling air-strikes) advance story and world-state understanding
Hub system connecting large outdoor areas to specific mission goals, communicating invasion strategy and tactical progression
Captured insane comrades visually indicate Strogg's threat to humanity and justify player's mission stakes
Visible damage states on creatures communicate combat intensity and world's violent state
Human military bases vs. Strogg organic-mechanical hybrid structures visually express faction identity and technological philosophies

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Active aggression, territorial defense of structures and bases
Visible wounds manifest on body; physiological response to player attacks
Removable head serves as key item for progression; special mission significance
Non-hostile presence; no interaction possible; narrative function only
Solo mission execution requiring strategic objective completion without squad support

notes: Quake II establishes world-state through mission-based narrative progression rather than persistent environmental changes. Faction identity is communicated through design (human military tech vs. Strogg bio-mechanical hybrid), and danger is signaled through enemy presence, environmental hazards, and fortification density. The game uses visible enemy damage as a key manifestation mechanic, creating feedback on world-state combat intensity. The isolated protagonist narrative and captured-comrade NPCs anchor the geopolitical stakes of human-Strogg conflict.
